title: Accelerated Vesting Agreement
description: Outlines 'single trigger' or 'double trigger' acceleration of stock option vesting in M&A scenarios.
whenToUse: Use this executive option addendum to define vesting acceleration rights if the startup is acquired or the executive is terminated.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: ACCELERATED VESTING AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Accelerated Vesting Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Vesting Acceleration Rules
clauses.body: Executive {executive_name} is granted {acceleration_percentage}% vesting acceleration upon the occurrence of: {acceleration_trigger}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is a double-trigger acceleration?
faq.answer: It requires two events to trigger acceleration: (1) the company is acquired, AND (2) the executive is terminated without cause within a year.

faq.question: Why is double-trigger preferred over single-trigger?
faq.answer: Acquirers prefer double-triggers because it incentivizes the executive team to stay and assist with the post-acquisition integration.
